records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
8232
Flashcards
Who were the two sons of Chaudhary 
Santoshi?
Dhanaram and Dhairyanarayan.
8233
Flashcards
In the Dhairyanarayan lineage, who were 
the three sons?
Khadganarayan, Bhavanidatta, and Kalanath.
8234
Flashcards
What was the primary reason for the 
mass migration of residents from the 
village of Rashadh to Shishinagar 
between 1890 and 1895?
The contamination of the Haha Dhar river water 
led to a deadly epidemic of Kala-azar and other 
diseases.
8235
Flashcards
According to the genealogical records, 
which King of the Banaili Raj facilitated 
the establishment of Shishinagar for the 
displaced families of Rashadh?
Raja Bahadur Kirtyanand Singh.
8236
Flashcards
After whom was the village of 
Shishinagar named?
Panji Shivanand Jha of the Sarisabe Baghwans 
Mool.
8237
Flashcards
In the Mithila genealogical system, what 
does the term 'Mool' signify?
The original ancestral village or root lineage of a 
family.
8238
Flashcards
Who was the author of the 'Devanand 
Shakha Panji', a primary genealogical 
record mentioned in the text?
Devanand, also known as Choti Jha.
8239
Flashcards
Where is the 'Purushottam Shakha Panji' 
(originally from Mangalbani village) 
currently preserved?
Kameshwar Singh Sanskrit University.
8240
Flashcards
What distinguished the genealogical 
record maintained by Devanand (Choti 
Jha) from other records?
It specifically chronicled only the high-ranking 
'Satkulin' Brahmin lineages of that period.
8241
Flashcards
Panji Harshanand Jha was born in which 
year according to the recorded 
horoscopes?
1859 AD (or 1249 Sal Fasli).
8242
Flashcards
Where did Panji Bhikhya Jha travel to 
study the science of 'Panji' (genealogical 
record-keeping)?
The village of Koilakh.
8243
Flashcards
Under which scholar did Panji Bhikhya 
Jha undergo his long-term training in 
genealogy?
Shrikant, also known as Babue Mishra.
8244
Flashcards
Which historical event in 1937 saw 
Modanand Jha achieve the first-class 
first position in the 'Dhaut' examination 
for Panji Shastra?
The thread ceremony (Upanayana) of Maharaj 
Kumar Jiveshwar Singh of Darbhanga.
8245
Flashcards
Which professional field did Panji 
Panchanand Jha enter during the British 
administration?
He served as a 'Daroga' (Police Sub-Inspector).
8246
Flashcards
Identify the 'Mool' associated with the 
scholarly families of the village Oda 
Ekahara.
Madhura.
8247
Flashcards
What was the significance of the village 
Rashadh before the environmental crisis?
It was a renowned centre for scholars of Navy-
Nyaya, Dharmashastra, Grammar, and Astrology.
8248
Flashcards
The Panji records identify Panji Anand 
Jha as an expert in which specific field?
Panji-shastra (Genealogical Science).
8249
Flashcards
Who was the mother of Panji Jhulanand 
Jha?
Sitavati Devi.
8250
Flashcards
Which lineage (Mool) does the family of 
Professor Dr Umesh Kumar Jha from 
Vidhyapatinagar belong to?
Karamhe Rouni.
8251
Flashcards
Name the specific branch of the Haha 
Dhar river that was blamed for the 
decline of the Rashadh settlement.
A major branch of the Kosi River.
8252
Flashcards
According to the text, which scholar 
from the Bahirwad Parhat Mool was 
considered a 'son of Saraswati' and 
became the Karta-putra of Maharani 
Indravati?
Shri Mani Thakur.
8253
Flashcards
In the context of Mithila genealogy, what 
is a 'Panchobh' or 'Khaual'?
Sub-classifications or branches related to 
specific village lineages.
8254
Flashcards
What was the common physical ailment 
(epidemic) that forced the abandonment 
of Rashadh village?
Piphaliya disease (Splenomegaly) and Kala-azar.
8255
Flashcards
Which Panjikar is noted for migrating to 
Shishinagar at the age of thirty-five?
Panji Harshanand Jha.
8256
Flashcards
What awards did Modanand Jha receive 
from the Maharaja of Darbhanga for his 
excellence in Panji Shastra?
Two pairs of dhotis, a doshala (shawl), and a 
certificate of merit.
8257
Flashcards
The village Shishinagar gained 
widespread fame within a few years of 
its founding primarily because it was the 
residence of _____.
The Panjikars (Genealogists).
8258
Flashcards
Which member of the family is described 
as a prominent Congress social worker 
and 'Param Naishthik'?
Shyamsundar Jha.
8259
Flashcards
Who served as the Vice President of 
Police and held a B.A. degree in the 
lineage of Shukdev Jha?
Naresh Mohan.
8260
Flashcards
The 'Oda Ekahara' lineage includes 
families from which modern district 
region according to the village lists?
Purnia (specifically the Dharmapur province).
8261
Flashcards
Identify the maternal grandfather of 
Panji Jhulanand Jha from the village of 
Chanpura.
Tulsidatt Chaudhary of the Nahas Khaual Mool.
8262
Flashcards
Which branch of the Panji records is 
stated to be currently under the 
protection of the book's author?
The Devanand Shakha Panji.
8263
Flashcards
What was the specific location in 
Shishinagar where cultural and political 
meetings (Baithaks) were held during the 
independence struggle?
The temple of the village deity, Nrisingha 
(Nrisingha-sthan).
8264
Flashcards
Who was the father of the genealogist 
Harshanand Jha?
Dolanand, also known as Bhikhya Jha.
8265
Flashcards
Which scholar is mentioned as a famous 
Ayurveda practitioner and 'Nadi' (pulse) 
specialist in Shishinagar?
Apuch Jha.
8266
Flashcards
What was the occupation of 
Dhruvkumar Jha, son of Prajapati Jha?
Intelligence Department officer of the Central 
Government.
8267
Flashcards
Identify the 'Mool' of the family residing 
in the village of Parwaha (Loam 
Surugan).
Sarisabe Baghwans.
8268
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Shripati Jha 
mentioned in the Oda Ekahara lineage?
Shankaracharya and Bhanu.
8269
Flashcards
The text mentions a music mandali 
(group) formed in 1935 in Shishinagar; 
name one of its prominent members.
Girijanand Jha (Mahatma Ji).
8270
Flashcards
In the lineage of Barari, who were the 
two sons of Barinath Jha?
Kedarnath and Vishwanath.
8271
Flashcards
Which member of the Madhura Mool 
reached the rank of Bank Officer in 
Navratan Purnia?
Ganeshmohan, also known as Bhanu.
8272
Flashcards
Identify the 'Mool' of the families from 
the village of Asura in Shripur Desh.
Alaiyar.
8273
Flashcards
Which Panjikar was the first to construct 
the Devanand Shakha Panji?
Devanand (Choti Jha).
8274
Flashcards
What was the marital connection 
between Panji Bhikhya Jha and Panji 
Lutan Jha of Saurath?
They were brothers-in-law (Mausa connection).
8275
Flashcards
Who was the scholarly ancestor who 
migrated to Rashadh after receiving 
favour from Raja Ramchandra Narayan 
Rai of Pahansara?
Panji Anand Jha.
8276
Flashcards
According to the Panji system, what is 
the significance of the village 'Koilakh'?
It is a central authority for verifying genealogical 
records and marriage eligibility (Siddhanta).
8277
Flashcards
Which King's reign was marked by the 
'Brahma-hatya' (killing of a Brahmin) 
stigma, leading scholars to migrate to 
eastern Mithila?
Maharaj Narendra Singh.
8278
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Panji Choti Jha's 
daughter Ambika, who married into the 
Baghwans branch.
Jaykrishna and Choti.
8279
Flashcards
What is the primary language used for 
the traditional Panji records as described 
in the source?
Sanskrit mixed with Maithili.
8280
Flashcards
Which specific branch of knowledge did 
Panji Bhikhya Jha study to the exclusion 
of other subjects during his early 
training?
Panji Vidya (Genealogical Science).
8281
Flashcards
In the Shishinagar community, which 
family member was the first to receive 
English education and pass the entrance 
exam?
Tekmani Thakur.
8282
Flashcards
Identify the ancestor in the Oda Ekahara 
lineage who was a famous writer 
(Sahityakar).
Professor Mayananand Jha.
8283
Flashcards
Which son of Panji Bhikhya Jha served 
as a primary teacher (Pradhan Adhyapak) 
and held a B.A. Dip-in-Ed?
Bhagwan Jha.
8284
Flashcards
The village of Barari is associated with 
which primary 'Mool' branch according 
to the text?
Pahi Jijwal.
8285
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Shaktinath Jha listed 
in the Sultanpur lineage?
Virendra and Rajkumar.
8286
Flashcards
Which family member attained the 
position of High Court Chief Justice 
(Mukhya Nyayadhish)?
Lakshmikant Jha.
8287
Flashcards
In the lineage of Rucholi Madhura, who 
were the sons of Ganesh Jha?
Govind, Krishnaji, and Shambhunath.
8288
Flashcards
Which village served as the ancestral 
home of the genealogist Vidhyanand, 
also known as Mohanji?
Shishinagar.
8289
Flashcards
The migration from Rashadh to 
Shishinagar involved the relocation of 
families across a distance of _____.
Four 'kosh' (approximately 12 kilometres).
8290
Flashcards
Who was the maternal grandfather of 
King Kirtyanand Singh of Banaili?
Gopinath Jha, son of Yadunath Jha of the 
Darihara Ratauli Mool.
8291
Flashcards
Which Panjikar was known as a 
specialist in 'Navya Nyaya' (New Logic)?
Panji Harishwar Mishra.
8292
Flashcards
Identify the scholar from the 
Mahindrapur Pandua Mool who was an 
expert in the Panji Shastra.
Panji Anand Jha.
8293
Flashcards
Which female descendant is recorded as 
having married into the 'Malangia 
Kujoli' Mool?
Gangaday.
8294
Flashcards
What was the historical name of the 
region where Shripur and Sultanpur are 
located?
Dharmapur Desh.
8295
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Panji Harshanand 
Jha mentioned in the records?
Modanand, Ambikanand, and Shamananda.
8296
Flashcards
The text lists various professions held by 
descendants; name a technical profession 
frequently mentioned.
Engineer (Abhiyanta).
8297
Flashcards
In the Panji system, what document is 
traditionally consulted to verify if a 
marriage is 'Siddh' (eligible)?
The Shakha Panji (Branch Records).
8298
Flashcards
Which village is identified as the 
residence of the 'Arail Haria' branch of 
the Oda Ekahara Mool?
Vishnupur.
8299
Flashcards
In which year was the marriage of the 
genealogist Modanand Jha solemnised?
1938
8300
Flashcards
Who was the wife of Modanand Jha and 
the daughter of Mukund Jha?
Rameshwari Devi
8301
Flashcards
How many sons did Modanand Jha and 
Rameshwari Devi have?
Seven
8302
Flashcards
Which specific branch of knowledge did 
Modanand Jha dedicate his entire life to?
Panji Shastra (genealogy)
8303
Flashcards
Who was the professor at C.M. College, 
Darbhanga, who served as a mentor to 
Modanand Jha?
Ramanath Jha
8304
Flashcards
What is the title of the genealogical 
book written by Modanand Jha under the 
guidance of Ramanath Jha?
Alay Kul Prakash
8305
Flashcards
Modanand Jha authored a 700-page 
work on genealogy titled _____.
Nagram
8306
Flashcards
Which institution in Patna honoured 
Modanand Jha as a 'Distinguished 
Scholar'?
Maithili Academy
8307
Flashcards
What traditional Maithil honours were 
bestowed upon Modanand Jha alongside 
a copper plate (Tamrapatra)?
Pag and Shal
8308
Flashcards
According to the text, on which date did 
Modanand Jha pass away?
1998-11-14 00:00:00
8309
Flashcards
In which month and year did 
Rameshwari Devi, the wife of 
Modanand Jha, die?
1991-12-01 00:00:00

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
8310
Flashcards
Who is the genealogist from Shivinagar, 
Purnia, identified as a disciple of 
Modanand Jha?
Vidyanand Jha
8311
Flashcards
Name the genealogist from Saurath who 
is part of Modanand Jha's disciple 
tradition.
Dr Kalika Dutt Jha
8312
Flashcards
What is the birth date of Panjikara 
Vidyanand Jha (also known as Mohan 
Ji)?
1957-04-09 00:00:00
8313
Flashcards
Which work by Vidyanand Jha covers 
the genealogical history from 450 AD to 
2008?
Genome Mapping
8314
Flashcards
Vidyanand Jha served as the co-editor of 
which Maithili magazine?
Videh Sadeh
8315
Flashcards
The genealogist Vidyanand Jha passed 
which traditional examination under 
Modanand Jha?
Dhauta Pariksha
8316
Flashcards
Niranjan Kumar, the grandson of 
Modanand Jha, is known for his role as 
an announcer (Udghoshak) for which 
organisation?
Akashvani (All India Radio)
8317
Flashcards
What is the professional occupation of 
Chandrashekhar Jha, the son of 
Lakshminandan Jha?
Bank Official
8318
Flashcards
Which son of Modanand Jha holds an 
LLM degree and has the alias 'Pintu'?
Saketanand
8319
Flashcards
Which son of Modanand Jha practised as 
a Homeopathic doctor?
Harinandan
8320
Flashcards
Saryug Lal, mentioned in the records of 
Bhadreshwar, held which ministerial 
position in the Bihar government?
Health Minister
8321
Flashcards
Punyanand, a former MLA and scholar, 
is the author of which historical book?
Mithila Darpan
8322
Flashcards
In Maithil genealogy, what term is used 
to refer to the ancestral root village of a 
lineage?
Mula Grama
8323
Flashcards
Who was the maternal grandfather 
(Nana) of Modanand Jha's wife, 
Rameshwari Devi?
M.M.U. Tumbe Jha
8324
Flashcards
What was the name of Modanand Jha's 
elder daughter?
Meena
8325
Flashcards
Which son of Modanand Jha is described 
as a 'Highly Capable Teacher' (Yogyatam 
Adhyapak) with an MA degree?
Lakshminandan
8326
Flashcards
Concept: Panjikara
Definition: A traditional specialist or registrar 
who maintains the complex genealogical records 
(Panji) of the Maithil Brahmin community.
8327
Flashcards
Identify the son of Modanand Jha who 
was born on the Tuesday of Chaitra 
Shukla Navami, 1957.
Vidyanand Jha
8328
Flashcards
Which relative of Modanand Jha is noted 
for being a Public Prosecutor (Lok 
Abhiyojak) in Katihar?
Leelanand Jha
8329
Flashcards
What is the primary subject matter of the 
book 'Maithil Brahmanon ki Panji 
Vyavastha'?
The systematic structure of genealogical records 
among Maithil Brahmins.
8330
Flashcards
Who was the father of Modanand Jha's 
son-in-law, Professor Dr Vishwambhar 
Mishra 'Vagish'?
Satyanarayan Mishra
8331
Flashcards
Which ancestral root (Mula) is 
associated with the lineage starting with 
'Keshav' and 'Narayan'?
Surgan Pindokhar
8332
Flashcards
In the records of Sodarpur, who is the 
son of Vastu who had two sons named 
Ram and Hari?
Shyamkanth
8333
Flashcards
Which son of Modanand Jha is 
associated with the alias 'Boua'?
Janakinandan
8334
Flashcards
In the lineage of Madiyaari, who are the 
three sons of Buddhisena?
Shulapani, Gadh, and Belounch
8335
Flashcards
Which descendant of the Madiyaari root 
served as a retired Director of the Bihar 
State Bridge Construction Corporation?
Amlendu Kumar
8336
Flashcards
Who is the author of 'Genome Mapping 
Volume II', as mentioned in the 
Vidyanand Jha section?
Vidyanand Jha
8337
Flashcards
What is the Mula Grama (root village) 
of the genealogical records starting with 
'Mahamahopadhyay Jiveshwar'?
Sodarpur Suhatthari
8338
Flashcards
The document notes that Modanand Jha 
received a certificate of 'Sadhana Seva' 
from which institute in Darbhanga?
Mithila Sanskrit Post-Graduate Research 
Institute
8339
Flashcards
In the lineage of Modanand Jha, which 
grandson is an engineer named Pushkar?
Pushkar (son of Meena)
8340
Flashcards
Which son of Shardanand is noted for 
his academic background with a BA 
degree and the nickname 'Bablu'?
Gaurinandan
8341
Flashcards
Who is the son of Lakshminandan Jha 
that works as an officer for Akashvani?
Niranjan Kumar
8342
Flashcards
Which location in Purnia is currently the 
residence of Panjikara Vidyanand Jha?
Maithil Tol
8343
Flashcards
Who were the two daughters of 
Modanand Jha mentioned in the text?
Lalita (Meena) and Navina
8344
Flashcards
The genealogist Modanand Jha is 
frequently referred to by the honorific 
title _____.
Panji Shastra Martand
8345
Flashcards
Which scholar is credited with writing 
'Alay Kul Prakash' along with Modanand 
Jha?
Professor Ramanath Jha
8346
Flashcards
Who is the father of the genealogist 
Vidyanand Jha?
Modanand Jha
8347
Flashcards
According to the genealogical table, who 
is the son of Shardanand with the alias 
'Mantu'?
Diwakar
8348
Flashcards
Who was the father of Rameshwari 
Devi, the wife of Modanand Jha?
Mukund Jha
8349
Flashcards
What was the date of Vidyanand Jha's 
appointment or association with Maithil 
Tol, Purnia?
1987-07-01 00:00:00
8350
Flashcards
Which Maithil root (Mula) is described 
as 'Sultanpur Deshe Bhadreshwar'?
Bhadreshwar
8351
Flashcards
Who is the father of the retired director 
Amlendu Kumar mentioned in the 
records?
Mahanand
8352
Flashcards
What is the alias of Janakinandan, the 
youngest son of Modanand Jha?
Boua
8353
Flashcards
In the genealogical records, what does 
the abbreviation 'M.M.U.' typically stand 
for?
Mahamahopadhyay (a title for a great scholar)
8354
Flashcards
Who is identified in the text as the 
author of the work 'Kapal Kundala'?
Shivanand
8355
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Shivanand, the 
author of 'Kapal Kundala'.
Kamlanand and Balkrishna
8356
Flashcards
Who is the father of Shivanand 
according to the Dumariya genealogical 
records?
Babulal
8357
Flashcards
Identify the father of Avadhesh.
Kamlanand
8358
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Avadhesh from 
the Kahra and Rajanpura records.
Manoj and Neeraj
8359
Flashcards
Who is the father of Naresh, a resident 
of Vishnupur in the Asja region?
Balkrishna
8360
Flashcards
What is the name of Naresh's son?
Ambuj
8361
Flashcards
Identify the profession and degree 
associated with Ashok Kumar in the 
Dumariya records.
He was a Bank Manager with an M.Sc. degree.
8362
Flashcards
Who is the father of Amit (also known 
as Sonu)?
Ashok Kumar
8363
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Dilip from the 
Dumariya lineage.
Gautam and Govind
8364
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Devanarayan?
Arun, Varun, Ashok, Dilip, Sushil and Subodh
8365
Flashcards
In the records of Nikhooti Barhi 
Dumariya, who are the four sons of 
Nunnu?
Muktinath, Teerthnath, Astanand and Nityanand
8366
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Teerthnath 
residing in Radheli.
Atul Kumar and Shardanand
8367
Flashcards
Who is the father of Phanindra and 
Girindra?
Astanand
8368
Flashcards
Identify the son of Nityanand mentioned 
in the Nikhooti records.
Gajendra
8369
Flashcards
Who are the three sons of Pritinath?
Khelandand, Dhiranand and Mantook
8370
Flashcards
The son of Mantu from the Simratala 
region is _____.
Phul
8371
Flashcards
In the Jahanpur records, who is the father 
of the four brothers Amarendra, Kailash, 
Trilok and Ananta?
Abhyanand
8372
Flashcards
Name the son of Amarendra mentioned 
in the Jahanpur records.
Manish
8373
Flashcards
Identify the son of Kailashnath who 
resides in Jhanjharpur.
Mukesh
8374
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Trilok?
Nitesh and Rahul
8375
Flashcards
In the records of Pabulidhad Badhiyam, 
who is the son of Maheshnath?
Jagdish
8376
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Jagdish.
Subodh and Pramod
8377
Flashcards
Who are the three sons of Gunmani?
Kamleshwar, Sarveshwar and Deveshwar
8378
Flashcards
Identify the son of Kamleshwar, also 
known by the name Phatkan.
Virenra
8379
Flashcards
In the records of Gangi Dighoy, who is 
the father of Ravindranath?
Satyanarayan
8380
Flashcards
Name the five sons of Ravindranath 
from Gangi Dighoy.
Vishwambhar, Ajay, Vimal, Pratap and Rajesh
8381
Flashcards
Who is the father of the four brothers 
Gangadhar, Muralidhar, Jalandhar and 
Kapildhar?
Nevalal
8382
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Muralidhar.
Manoj and Anil
8383
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Jalandhar.
Rinku and Pinku
8384
Flashcards
In the records of Harda Ghat, who is the 
father of Fakir?
Lakshman
8385
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Abhiyanta 
Manikant.
Prakash Kumar and Abhiyanta Shiv Kumar
8386
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Bhavichhan 
according to the Sirhanda village 
records?
Dinanath, Babunath and Yogananda
8387
Flashcards
Identify the sons of Jayagovind in the 
Sirhanda records.
Arvind, Vinod and Pramod
8388
Flashcards
Who is the son of Babunath, a resident 
of Bahadurganj?
Pushpnarayan
8389
Flashcards
Name the sons of Shatrughnanath from 
the Sakuri Pali records.
Prabhakar, Diwakar, Vibhakar and Sudhakar
8390
Flashcards
In the records of Nemua, who are the 
two sons of Palat?
Mohan and Mukund
8391
Flashcards
Who is the son of Mukund in the Nemua 
village records?
Chhatuk
8392
Flashcards
Identify the father of Santoshi and 
Mansukh.
Chhatuk
8393
Flashcards
Who is the son of Santoshi?
Harishwar
8394
Flashcards
Name the children of Harishwar 
mentioned in the records.
Tribhuvan, Jhonti, Keshi, Kaushiki, Chhabnu 
and Kalidatta
8395
Flashcards
In the Maithil Tola, Purnia records, who 
is the father of Madhav and Uddhav?
Kripashankar
8396
Flashcards
Name the son of Madhav from Maithil 
Tola.
Naitik Satyam
8397
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Ramakant in the 
Purnia records?
Umashankar, Gaurishankar, Mahashankar, 
Brajshankar and Jayshankar
8398
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Umashankar.
Avadhesh Vats and Vineet Vats
8399
Flashcards
Who is the son of Mahashankar?
Nilesh
8400
Flashcards
Name the son of Brajshankar.
Sameer
8401
Flashcards
In the Kamalpur records, who are the 
two sons of Shulapani?
Sabhyapati (Dani) and Kaushikidatt
8402
Flashcards
Identify the son of Sabhyapati (Dani).
Nayansukh
8403
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Kaushikidatt?
Bhavanidatt and Shyamlal
8404
Flashcards
Name the son of Shyamlal.
Babulal

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
8405
Flashcards
Who is the son of Babulal in the 
Kamalpur records?
Rajivalal
8406
Flashcards
Identify the son of Bhavanidatt.
Modnath
8407
Flashcards
Name the son of Modnath.
Sitalal
8408
Flashcards
Who is the son of Sitalal?
Khushilal
8409
Flashcards
Identify the son of Khushilal.
Uditlal
8410
Flashcards
In the records of Shahbajpur, who are the 
six sons of Vaitoo?
Raghunandan, Shivnandan, Devnandan, Sarvlal, 
Vishwanath and Vidyananda
8411
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Shivnandan?
Ramakant and Yogananda
8412
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Ramakant.
Krishnakant and Lalitnarayan
8413
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Yogananda.
Vijaykant and Ajaykant
8414
Flashcards
Who is the son of Devnandan?

are the sons of Ganesh?

Avadesh)

Flashcards
Who is the individual in the Birin root

worker) and 'Rajnitik Karyakarta' 
(political activist)?
Lalan
9558
Flashcards
In the Ganesh lineage of Pagdera, who 
are the three sons of Bhagwan?
Suman, Bhuvan, and Ramna
9559
Flashcards
Who are the four sons of Ganesh in the 
Khaual Mahua branch?
Mahanand, Parmanand, Krupanand, and 
Durganand
9560
Flashcards
In the Mandar Katouna Pagdera root, 
who is the father of Punyanand and 
Govind?
Mangalanand
9561
Flashcards
Who is the father of Kaustubh Kumar 
(MCA) in the Palasini lineage?
Padmanand
9562
Flashcards
In the Sijhuar Mool, what is the name of 
the branch originating from Budhwal 
Gangora?
Tamrakunda
9563
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Jaybodh in the 
Budhwal Gangora branch?
Shidhar and Shalandhar
9564
Flashcards
In the Palasini root, who is the son of 
Anmol in the Sulthanpur branch?
Baidyanath and Pashupatinath
9565
Flashcards
The branch of Sodarpur Digoun belongs 
to the _____ root.
Sijhuar
9566
Flashcards
In the 'Soderpur Pagdera' lineage, who 
are the sons of Jayanarayan and 
Shivanarayan?
Kapil, Bhideva, and Lalit.
9567
Flashcards
Which specific village is associated with 
the 'Triliyas Budheb' Mool?
Dhaulat (Madhepura).
9568
Flashcards
Who was the father of Narayandatta in 
the 'Triliyas Budheb' lineage?
Gangadatta.
9569
Flashcards
Name the four sons of Sagnan from the 
Triliyas Budheb branch.
Jagannath, Upendra, Bhupendra, and Amarendra.
9570
Flashcards
What is the notable religious affiliation 
of Acharya Mahendra of Gandhi Nagar, 
Purnea?
He is a prominent disciple of Dwarika Peeth 
Shankaracharya Swarupananda Saraswati.
9571
Flashcards
In the 'Soderpur Sarisab Shalampur' 
branch, who is the father of Khagesh, 
Naresh, Varun, and Kuber?
Motikant.
9572
Flashcards
Identify the military and professional 
background of Haridev from Maithil 
Tole, Purnea.
He is a retired Indian Air Force officer, an 
advocate (B.A., B.L.), and a decorated social 
thinker.
9573
Flashcards
Where does the engineer Anant Kumar, 
son of Haridev, currently reside?
The United States (America).
9574
Flashcards
In the 'Alayiar Usrauli' branch, who is 
the father of Bhulal and Dayal?
Jagannath.
9575
Flashcards
Amogh Narayan 'Amogh' is recognised 
as a scholar in which field?
Hindi Literature.
9576
Flashcards
Which school is associated with the 
teaching career of the scholar Amogh 
Narayan?
Lalji High School, Raniganj, Araria.
9577
Flashcards
Who is the retired Principal of the 
District School, Purnea, mentioned in 
the 'Bahera-Dhee Deep' Mool?
Bhasheshmohan.
9578
Flashcards
Name the three sons of Madhav in the 
'Soderpur Teghrihar' branch.
Ashok Kumar, Arvind Kumar, and Ajay Kumar.
9579
Flashcards
What is the profession of Arvind Kumar 
in the 'Soderpur Teghrihar' branch?
Bihar Administrative Service (B.P.S.) officer.
9580
Flashcards
Professor Rurpati of the 'Palitwar 
Samauli' branch is the son of which 
individual?
Advocate Janardan.
9581
Flashcards
In the 'Palitwar Samauli' lineage, who 
are the three sons of Chumman?
Jagdish, Kamalakant, and Vimalnath.
9582
Flashcards
What professional title is held by 
Jagdish, the son of Chumman?
Forest Conservation Officer (B.Sc. Honours).
9583
Flashcards
The lineage of Triloknath, Mananath, 
and Parashnath belongs to which branch 
in Purnea?
Sakradihi Titail Ramnagar branch (Maithil Tole).
9584
Flashcards
In the 'Mandar Siholi' branch, who are 
the sons of Chandraman?
Ashok and Manoj.
9585
Flashcards
Term: Suta (à¤¸à¥ à¤¿)
Definition: A term used in genealogical texts to 
denote a 'son'.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:

Dular.

Baidyanath and Dayanath.

Flashcards

Ghanshyam, Dukharan, and Maniram.

Flashcards

Flashcards

Flashcards
Who are the sons of Mati Nath in the

Shiromani and Govardhan.
9616
Flashcards
In the 'Rangpura' branch, who are the

Harinarayan and Ramnarayan.

Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Nandishwar in

Balbhar and Kamadev.
9618
Flashcards

Flashcards
In the 'Kuadi' branch, who are the three

Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Budhhinath in

Flashcards

Sadashiv.

Flashcards

Amal Kumar

Flashcards

Lalji

Flashcards

the _____ (administrative service).
B.P.S.

Flashcards
Concept: Mool (à¤®à¥‚à¤²)
Definition: The ancestral village or original root 
of a Maithil Brahmin family used to track 
lineage.

Advocate Haridev.

Anantaram.

Flashcards

Flashcards

Flashcards

Lalit.
9642
Flashcards

Narayandatta.
9643
Flashcards

Bahoran, Yadunandan, Jayanand and Harinandan.

Flashcards
Identify the maternal origin (dau) for the 
sons of Mansaram.

Flashcards

Manranjan and Kouskidatta.
9646

Flashcards

9650
Flashcards
Who are the four sons of Mansaram who

Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Meghnath

9652
Flashcards

Who are the two sons of Sahebram?

9656
Flashcards

9658
Flashcards

Ramdutta and Tulsiram.
9659
Flashcards

Tikaram, Atmaram, Kokil, Dattaram, Shivadutta

9660
Flashcards
Identify the four sons of Rajkumar

9661
Flashcards

Dr. Prof. Ranjit Singh.
9662
Flashcards
Who is the Rai Bahadur mentioned in 
the Ganauli branch?
Sitrinath.
9663
Flashcards
Which village is the primary residence

Sabour.
9664
Flashcards

9665
Flashcards
Who is the father of the four sons

Ramsundar.
9666
Flashcards
Identify the maternal origin (dau) for the 
sons of Ramsundar.
Nishikant from the Behat Khandwala clan.
9667
Flashcards
Who are the three sons of Sarangdhar 
mentioned in the Pavanchob section?
Ruranand, Jhulanand and Yugal.
9668
Flashcards
Who are the four sons of Anand

Ram, Lakshman, Bhoganand and Nityanand.
9669
Flashcards
Name the two sons of Mahanand 
mentioned in the Barantpur branch.
Gunanand and Yoganand.
9670
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Umanath (also

Gopikant, Vishnukant, Suryakant, Chandrakant 
and Mayakant.
9671
Flashcards
Which clan provided the maternal origin

9672
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Bechan in the

Shambhunath, Dinesh, Vinesha, Gandhesha and

9673
Flashcards
Who are the four sons of Mahesh, son of 
Chhinath?
Dharmanath, Brahmanand, Karmanand and 
Sarvananda.
9674
Flashcards
Identify the maternal origin (dau) for 
Padmakar (also known as Amirilaal).
MangalÅ« from the Pavanchob clan.
9675
Flashcards
Name the four sons of Amirilaal in the

Digambar, Shivakant, Sanjay and Manoj.
9676
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Balbodh in the 
Rajoura branch?
Anirudh, Kashinath, Devnath, Premnath and 
Srinath.
9677
Flashcards
Which maternal origin (dau) is 
associated with the sons of Balbodh?
Jhingur from the Karmaha clan.
9678
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Krishnabodh in 
the Rajoura branch?
Amarnath, Rajnath, Vinod, Hareram and 
Parashuram.
9679
Flashcards
Identify the maternal origin (dau) for the 
sons of Krishnabodh.
Shilanath from the Hariaam clan.
9680
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Vasudev in the 
Katouna branch?
Upendra, Mahendra, Virendra, Devendra and 
Dilip.
9681
Flashcards
Name the three sons of Vishwanath in 
the Belunch Kako branch.
Jatadhar, Gangadhar and Muralidhar.
9682
Flashcards
Who are the three sons of Kapileshwar 
in the Alay branch?
Yugeshwar, Rameshwar and Kameshwar.
9683
Flashcards
Identify the maternal origin (dau) for the 
sons of Kapileshwar.
Jinilaal from the Kujouli clan.
9684
Flashcards
Who are the four sons of Batesh in the 
Hariaam branch?
Lakshmikant, Shobhakant, Suryakant and 
Ramakant.
9685
Flashcards
Who are the three sons of Balbodh in the 
Narsam branch of North Toll?
Ashok, Sarayug and Shankar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
9686
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Jayadev in the 
Kokilad branch?
Ramdev and Krishnadev.
9687
Flashcards
Identify the three sons of Bouku in the 
Ghusouit branch.
Chaturanand, Mathuranand and Devanand.
9688
Flashcards
Who are the four sons of Balkishor in 
the Belunch Kako branch of North Toll?
Shivanand, Chaturanand, Dayanand and 
Ganganand.
9689
Flashcards
Name the four sons of Yuktinath in the 
Hariaam Rakhwari branch.
Dayakant, Vedakant, Vidyakant and Tarakant.
9690
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Padmakar in 
the Sarisab Khangur branch?
Vijay Shankar, Ajay Shankar, Sudhir Shankar, 
Vikash Shankar and Abhay Shankar.
9691
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Dukhmochan in 
the Sodarpur branch.
Gauri Shankar and Kalyan Shankar.
9692
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Mukund in the 
Kalhua village section?
Baidyanath and Kripanath.
9693
Flashcards
Identify the maternal origin (dau) for the 
sons of Mukund.
Damodar from the Rauali Darihara clan.
9694
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Udit in the 
Simhashram branch?
Baidyanath, Jagannath, Shambhunath, Shivanath 
and Shyamnath.
9695
Flashcards
Name the five sons of Sunderlal 
mentioned in the text.
Manmohan, Mahanand, Suryanand, Jhulanand 
and Durganand.
9696
Flashcards
Who are the four sons of Dinanath 
(descendant of Maddy) in the Banaili 
branch?
Chaturanand, Mathuranand, Devanand and 
Dinanath.
9697
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Sarvajlal in the 
Mandar Kansam branch?
Avadhkishor, Jaykishor, Harikishor, Shrikishor 
and Rajkishor.
9698
Flashcards
Identify the maternal origin (dau) for the 
sons of Sarvajlal.
Kankir from the Barkhanda clan.
9699
Flashcards
Who are the five sons of Jhapat in the 
Sodarpur Raiyam branch?
Girijanand, Ramanand, Chaturanand, Dayanand 
and Panchananda.
9700
Flashcards
Which clan provided the maternal origin 
(dau) for the sons of Jhapat?
Ranglal from the Nikhuti clan.
9701
Flashcards
Who are the three sons of Nandmohan in 
the Rauali branch?
Shashimohan, Rajmohan and Lalmohan.
9702
Flashcards
What is the primary academic focus of 
the genealogical material provided in the 
source?
The lineages (Mools) and family trees of the 
Maithil Brahmin community.
9703
Flashcards
In the context of the Sodarpur Sarisab 
lineage, who are the four sons of 
Panchnath?
Damodar, Sridhar, Murlidhar, and Chakradhar.
9704
Flashcards
Who is the father of Grijanand within 
the Sodarpur lineage?
Damodar.
9705
Flashcards
Name the individual described as a 
specialist professor for IAS officers in 
Mussoorie and an expert in Political 
Science.
Dr Professor Jagdish Chandra.
9706
Flashcards
Which lineage is associated with the 
'Patanaliwad Hati' mool and has a 
presence in the Chanmari Market of 
Kolkata?
Mool Belur.
9707
Flashcards
According to the source, who authored 
the book 'Migration and Achievement of 
Maithil Panditas'?
Dr Professor Jagdish Chandra.
9708
Flashcards
What professional role did Abhash 
Kumar, son of Dr Jagdish Chandra, hold 
at the World Bank?
Economic Advisor.
9709
Flashcards
Which individual is identified as a 
'Nationally Renowned Journalist' 
(Rashtriya Khyatiprapt Patrakar) and 
author?
Subhash K. Anand.
9710
Flashcards
In the lineage of 'Kusouli Ullu', which 
resident of Padri (Saharsa) is noted as 
holding an MA, PhD, and BL?
Shankarnath.
9711
Flashcards
Who is the father of the brothers 
Baidyanath and Shivnath in the 
Pavanchobh Virpur lineage?
Gunanand.
9712
Flashcards
The lineage 'Khauwal Simarwar' is 
primarily associated with which 
residential area?
Mahalbari.
9713
Flashcards
Which location is cited as the residence 
of the family of Shobhakanth, a Vakeel

Sipahi Tola, Purnea.
9714
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Grijanand listed 
as having 'Pr.' (Pratishtha/Alias) names

Vijendra and Ramendra.
9715
Flashcards
Which village is the primary seat for the 
'Khandwala Bhara' lineage?
Bhara (Bhawara).
9716
Flashcards
Who is the father of the four sons 
Lilmohan, Harimohan, Krishnamohan, 
and Rammohan?
Sridhar.
9717
Flashcards
In the records, what does the term 'Dau' 
(Dauhitra) typically signify regarding 
family connections?
The maternal grandfather's lineage or the 
connection via a daughter.
9718
Flashcards
Name the son of Damodar whose 
maternal grandfather was Janaklal.
Grijanand.
9719
Flashcards
Which professional title was held by 
Ishwar Chandra in the Professor Colony 
of Purnea before retirement?
Deputy Collector.
9720
Flashcards
Who is the father of the sons Ramesh, 
Ganesh, and Naresh in the Rameshwar 
lineage?
Manmohan.
9721
Flashcards
Which book title, other than those on 
migration, is attributed to Dr Jagdish 
Chandra in the Patna section?
Saath Samundar Paar.
9722
Flashcards
The lineage 'Mandar Brahmpur' is noted

Kothia (Jhanjharpur).
9723
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Yadunath in the 
'Khauwal Khadik' lineage?
Baldev, Sahadev, Shukdev, and Kapil Dev.
9724
Flashcards
Which individual served as the Director 
of Regional Doordarshan (Kshaitriya 
Doordarshan)?
Promod Kumar.
9725
Flashcards
In the 'Thariya Amrawati' lineage, what 
prefix is used for Aniruddha, son of 
Umapati?
Kaviraj.
9726
Flashcards
Which town is associated with the 
'Katihar City' genealogical entry?
Katihar.
9727
Flashcards
Who is the father of the brothers 
Jaiprakash and Balkrishna in the 
Radhakanth lineage?
Radhakanth.
9728
Flashcards
Which village is the residence of the 
'Jajwal Bhadgam' lineage in Subhash 
Nagar, Purnea?
Sirsiya.
9729
Flashcards

noted physician?
Dr Prof. Ganpat.
10516
Flashcards
What is the profession of Bhavanand, the 
son of Kartikeya in the Shrotriya lineage?
Engineer-in-Chief for the Government of Bihar.
10517
Flashcards
Who is the son of Bhavanand that serves 
as a UNICEF officer with an MBA?
Tushar Kumar.
10518
Flashcards
Which family member is noted for being 
a novelist (Yashasvini) with an MA 
degree?
Srimati Asha.
10519
Flashcards
Who is the 'Beeji' ancestor of the Jajwal 
Bhadgam lineage?
Manek.
10520
Flashcards
List the five sons of the founding 
ancestor Manek in the Jajwal lineage.
Shatikant, Ratikant, Mayurkant, Anantkant, and 
Manikant.
10521
Flashcards
Which individual served as the Advocate 
General of the Patna High Court and the 
Chairman of the Bihar Legislative 
Council from 2007-2012?
Tara Kant Jha.
10522
Flashcards
What significant linguistic achievement 
is attributed to Tara Kant Jha in the text?
He was instrumental in including the Maithili 
language in the Eighth Schedule of the Indian 
Constitution.
10523
Flashcards
According to the text, Tara Kant Jha is 
considered a leading activist for which 
cause?
The movement for a separate Mithila State.
10524
Flashcards
In the lineage of Jajwal, whose name 
was used to establish the 'Pachwar Par' 
Shrestha Panji?
Mahadev Jha (son of Anant Jha).
10525
Flashcards
Who is the 'Beeji' of the Khandwala 
(Kharaure) lineage?
Gangadhar.
10526
Flashcards
Which descendant of the Khandwala 
lineage is specifically noted as the 
founder (Upayaka) of the Khandwala 
village?
Sankarshana.
10527
Flashcards
Where is the Oini Mool (original 
lineage) geographically located 
according to the text?
Adhaidanga, Bengal.
10528
Flashcards
In the Oini lineage, who are the three 
sons of Bhawani?
Gangadhar, Rishikesh, and Krishni.
10529
Flashcards
Which professional designation is held 
by Dr Manoj Kumar in the Alay 
Nanyapur lineage?
MBBS (Doctor).
10530
Flashcards
In the Darrihara Badgam lineage, who is 
the son of Ramkar?
Harikar.
10531
Flashcards
Which specific branch of the family 
moved from Salampur to Lalbagh, 
Darbhanga?
The descendants of Mishra Shivi.
10532
Flashcards
Who are the sons of Shyam Lal in the 
Salampur-Darbhanga line?
Shri Raman and Raghunath.
10533
Flashcards
The son of Shri Raman in the Salampur 
lineage is named _____.
Aniruddha.
10534
Flashcards
In the Narsam lineage, who are the sons 
of Mishra Purushottam?
Raghuram, Gunakar, Ghanshyam, Dattaram, 
Purandar, and Lala.
10535
Flashcards
Which individual in the Jajwal lineage is 
described as a 'Kaviraj'?
Chhitai (son of Madhav).
10536
Flashcards
The son of Tara Kant Jha, who is also an 
advocate, is named _____.
Mantu.
10537
Flashcards
In the Soradpur Sarisab lineage, who are 
the two sons of Harihar?
Matikant and Mayakant.
10538
Flashcards
Who is the author of 'Mithila Itihas' 
(History of Mithila) as noted in the DDC 
Gajanand's description?
Gajanand.
10539
Flashcards
The ancestral village (Mool) of the 
physician Dr Ganpat is _____.
Salampur.
10540
Flashcards
In the Khandwala lineage, Sankarshana is 
famously known by which title?
Prasiddha Sankarshana.
10541
Flashcards
Who are the six sons of Shaktain 
(Shaun) in the Khandwala lineage?
Bhadreshwar, Damodar, Baikunth, Nilkanth, 
Shrikant, and Dhyankant.
10542
Flashcards
Which descendant of the Khandwala 
lineage is described as a 'Vishwa-
Vikhyat' (world-famous) person?
Ganesh.
10543
Flashcards
In the lineage of Oini, who is the son of 
HarirÄ m?
Raghunath.
10544
Flashcards
In the genealogy of Shivnagar, who is the 
father of the sons Shatikant and Ratikant?
Manek.
10545
Flashcards
Who is the son of Anant in the Siha 
Mool (Nalshira) lineage?
Bhagwan, Balbhadra, and Harinandan.
10546
Flashcards
In the Madhyanagar (Bengal) lineage, 
who is the father of Indranarayan?
Bhuvaneshwar.
10547
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Indranarayan in 
the Madhyanagar line.
Kashinath and Bholanath.
10548
Flashcards
Which son of Kashinath in the 
Madhyanagar lineage is associated with 
the Jagaur Soradpur branch?
Anand and Ishwarchandra.
10549
Flashcards
Who is the 'Beeji' of the Siha Mool 
lineage?
Shiva.
10550
Flashcards
In the Siha Mool line, who are the two 
sons of Shiva?
Haridash and Shrinath.
10551
Flashcards
Who in the Siha lineage is described as 
'Tantra Shiromani' and 'Agamacharya'?
Panchanand.
10552
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of the Tantra 
scholar Panchanand.
Sudhakar and Vasudev.
10553
Flashcards
In the Purnia Maithil Tole lineage, who 
serves as the Ward Commissioner?
Vivekanand alias Bablu.
10554
Flashcards
Who is the son of Vivekanand alias 
Bablu in the Purnia lineage?
Vaibhav.
10555
Flashcards
In the Bhadeshwar lineage, who are the 
sons of Khadganarayan?
Harikrishna, Chintamani, and Karu.
10556
Flashcards
What is the name of the daughter of Dr 
Manoj Kumar who is an LLB graduate?
Puja Vats.
10557
Flashcards
In the Baliyas Narsam lineage, who is 
the father of Achyuta, Narayan, 
Harikrishna, Ramkrishna, and Jagadish?
Mishra Raghav.
10558
Flashcards
Who is the father of Mishra Raghav in 
the Baliyas line?
Isharu.
10559
Flashcards
Which village is the original 'Mool' for 
the lineage of Purushottam mentioned at 
the start of the Tirhut section?
Baliyas Narsam.
10560
Flashcards
In the Salampur lineage, who is the son 
of Aniruddha?
Parmanand, Dayanand, and Shivanand.
10561
Flashcards
Which individual is identified as the son 
of Baidyanath in the Shrotriya branch of 
Salampur?
Bhavnath alias Kanhaiya.
10562
Flashcards
Who is the father of Shashinath, Rajnath, 
and Shambhunath in the Shrotriya line?
Baidyanath.
10563
Flashcards
In the Baliyas lineage, who are the sons 
of Mishra Purandar?
Padhadhar alias Nakatu, Karu, Shivu, Jeevi, and 
Bhavi.
10564
Flashcards
Which son of Nakatu is mentioned in the 
Baliyas lineage?
Soni and Poshan.
10565
Flashcards
Who are the daughters of Soni in the 
Baliyas Narsam line?
Bataha, Ishwaridatt, and Giridharika.
10566
Flashcards
In the Jajwal lineage, who is the son of 
Gopal?
Kanh.
10567
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Damodar alias 
Damu in the Jajwal line?
Pagu and Supan.
10568
Flashcards
In the Bhor Rajgram lineage, who are the 
three sons of Moti?
Baidyanath, Dinanath, and Devkinath.
10569
Flashcards
Who is the son of Kamalnayan in the 
Jajwal-Shivnagar branch?
Baburaiya and Atmaram.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
10570
Flashcards
In the Shivnagar lineage, who are the 
two sons of Atmaram?
Devidatt and Gauridatt.
10571
Flashcards
Which daughter of Atmaram is 
mentioned in the Shivnagar line?
Saryan, Tulsidatt, and Lakshmidatta.
10572
Flashcards
In the Patna branch, what is the 
professional title of Tara Kant?
Mahadhivakta (Advocate General).
10573
Flashcards
Who is identified as the father of 
Advocate Mantu in the Tara Kant 
lineage?
Tara Kant.
10574
Flashcards
In the Tulsia Kishanganj lineage, who 
are the two sons of Ratnakar?
Gaurikant and Shashikant.
10575
Flashcards
Who is the 'Beeji' of the Khandwala 
(Kharaure) Sarparab lineage?
Gangadhar.
10576
Flashcards
In the Khandwala branch, who are the 
sons of Sankarshana (Shaun)?
Bhadreshwar, Damodar, Baikunth, Nilkanth, 
Shrikant, and Dhyankant.
10577
Flashcards
Who is the father of the sons 
Grahamshwar and Bhogishwar in the 
Khandwala line?
Harikant.
10578
Flashcards
In the Madhyanagar branch of the Bengal 
Dhajoli region, who is the son of 
Bhavadev?
Shivadev and Lakshmidev.
10579
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Shivadev Jha in 
the Madhyanagar lineage.
Gopal and Gadadhar.
10580
Flashcards
Who is the son of Gadadhar Jha in the 
Madhyanagar line?
Jeevanath.
10581
Flashcards
Who is credited with the 'Bhamati' 
commentary on the Brahma Sutras in the 
Baliyas lineage records?
Mahamahopadhyaya Vachaspati.
10582
Flashcards
Identify the individual who served as the 
Chairman of the Bihar Legislative 
Council from 2007 to 2012.
Tarakant.
10583
Flashcards
Which language did Tarakant 
successfully advocate to include in the 
Eighth Schedule of the Indian 
Constitution?
Maithili.
10584
Flashcards
What was the professional designation 
of Bhavanand within the Government of 
Bihar?
Engineer-in-Chief.
10585
Flashcards
According to the Baliyas lineage, who is 
the great-grandson of the commentator 
Vachaspati?
Lakshminath.
10586
Flashcards
The commentator Vachaspati is famous 
for his work titled the _____.
Bhamati Tika.
10587
Flashcards
What is the primary 'Mool' (ancestral 
origin village) of Gajanand and 
Chandranand?
Sodarpur.
10588
Flashcards
Identify the famous physician and 
professor at Darbhanga Medical College 
mentioned in the text.
Dr Ganpati.
10589
Flashcards
Tarakant served as the _____ of the 
Bihar Legislative Council between 2007 
and 2012.
Chairman.
10590
Flashcards
In the context of the Jajwal lineage, 
which individual was a prominent 
Congress leader from Sirsiya village?
Shridev Jha.
10591
Flashcards
Which lineage origin is associated with 
the 'Shivnagar' branch in the Tirhut 
country?
Jajwal.
10592
Flashcards
What was the professional role of 
Gajanand (Sodarpur lineage) in the 
administrative services?
Deputy Development Commissioner (DDC).
10593
Flashcards
The individual Tushar Kumar is 
professionally associated with the 
international organisation _____.
UNICEF.
10594
Flashcards
Which novelist, known by the name 
'Yashaswini', is the daughter of Dr 
Ganpati?
Asha.
10595
Flashcards
Who is the son of Gajanand mentioned 
in the Sodarpur records?
Manas.
10596
Flashcards
The Baliyas lineage is originally 
associated with which geographic 
province?
Tirhut.
10597
Flashcards
Identify the son of Tarakant who is also 
noted as an advocate.
Mantu.
10598
Flashcards
In genealogical terminology, the term 
'Suta' refers to a _____.
Daughter.
10599
Flashcards
Which academic degree does the 
individual Vivek Kashyap hold?
Master of Arts (MA).
10600
Flashcards
Panchanand is noted in the records as a 
retired officer from which department?
Labour Department (Labour Superintendent).
10601
Flashcards
The branch of the Oini lineage located in 
Bengal is associated with which region?
Maldah.
10602
Flashcards
Identify the son of Bhavanand who 
worked as a UNICEF official.
Tushar Kumar.
10603
Flashcards
What is the specific 'Mool' associated 
with the branch in Shobhanagar, Bengal?
Darihara.
10604
Flashcards
In the Baliyas lineage, who is the son of 
Ratipati?
Shrikrishna.
10605
Flashcards
The individual Madan is described as a 
teacher of which subject?
Sanskrit.
10606
Flashcards
Which lineage does the Ward 
Commissioner Vivekanand (alias Bablu) 
belong to?
Darihara.
10607
Flashcards
Who was the father of the former 
Advocate General Tarakant?
Gayadatta.
10608
Flashcards
In the Jajwal lineage records, who is the 
son of Gopal?
Kanh.
10609
Flashcards
Identify the son of Shardanand who 
resided in the village of Mangarouni.
Baidyanath.
10610
Flashcards
In the Baliyas lineage, Bhairavdatt and 
Shankardatt are the sons of _____.
Padmanabh.
10611
Flashcards
Which lineage origin is associated with 
the 'Saraiyasha' region and Kishunpur 
village?
Visha.
10612
Flashcards
What is the educational qualification of 
Arun Kumar as listed in the Darihara 
records?
Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com).
10613
Flashcards
The individual Panchanand belongs to 
which specific Maithil Brahmin lineage?
Baliyas.
10614
Flashcards
Identify the son of Baidyanath from 
Mangarouni who is also known as 
Kanhaiya.
Bhavnath.
10615
Flashcards
In the Oini lineage, who is identified as 
the son of Hariram?
Raghunath.
10616
Flashcards
Which village in Purnia district became 
the home for the descendants of 
Darbhikh Jha?
Sirsiya.
10617
Flashcards
Who is the son of Mithlal mentioned in 
the Baliyas lineage?
Harikant.
10618
Flashcards
Identify the son of Jagannath in the Oini 
lineage records.
Kalicharan.
10619
Flashcards
The Sodarpur branch records mention 
that Sakma, daughter of Gajanand, lived 
in which village?
Paramgarh.
10620
Flashcards
Term: Mool
Definition: The ancestral root or village of 
origin for a Maithil Brahmin lineage.
10621
Flashcards
In the Jajwal lineage, which individual is 
the son of Damodar (alias Damu)?
Pagu.
10622
Flashcards
Who is the son of Pagu in the Jajwal 
lineage?
Juvadirath.
10623
Flashcards
Identify the daughter of Gajanand who is 
described as a resident of the village 
Alay.
Sakma.
10624
Flashcards
The individual Tushar Kumar obtained 
which professional degree before joining 
UNICEF?
Master of Business Administration (MBA).
10625
Flashcards
In the Baliyas lineage, who are the two 
sons of Arjun?
Baldev and Arjun himself (Wait, Arjun's sons 
are Arjun and Baldev).
10626
Flashcards
According to the Baliyas lineage, who 
are the sons of Chintamani (alias 
Laturun)?
Babejik.
10627
Flashcards
Who is the father of the Jajwal lineage 
member Murari Dhar (alias Dharu)?
Manahar.
10628
Flashcards
The individual Gayadatta is mentioned 
as the son of which person in the Jajwal 
lineage?
Jaldhar.
10629
Flashcards
In the Baliyas lineage, identify the three 
sons of Ram.
Vedanand, Shardanand, and Chandranand.
10630
Flashcards
Which professional field was 
Pashupatinath associated with?
Law (Advocate).
10631
Flashcards
The Oini lineage member Raghunath has 
how many sons mentioned in the 
records?
Three (Vinod, Santosh, and Sadanand).
10632
Flashcards
In the Darihara lineage, identify the son 
of Parashnath.
Ghoghan.
10633
Flashcards
Who are the two sons of Jagdish in the 
Darihara lineage?
Ramakant and Tulakant.
10634
Flashcards
Identify the 'Mool' of the branch located 
in Madhyanagar, Bengal.
Visha.
10635
Flashcards
The Sodarpur member Chintamani (alias 
Laturun) had which two daughters 
mentioned as 'Sahodara'?
Anupama and Jayapama.
10636
Flashcards
In the Jajwal lineage, who is the father of 
Jaldhar?
Gauridatta.
10637
Flashcards
Which lineage does the individual 
Baidyanath, who lived in Mangarouni, 
belong to?
Baliyas.
10638
Flashcards
The individual Shrikrishna is the son of 
_____ according to the Baliyas lineage.
Ratipati.
10639
Flashcards
In the Oini lineage, identify the son of 
'Kummar' Kartik.
Balaknath.
10640
Flashcards
What is the relationship between 
Bhavnand and Kartikeya?
Bhavnand is the son of Kartikeya.
10641
Flashcards
During which centuries did Maithil 
Brahmins primarily migrate to Bengal 
for livelihood?
The 16th to the 18th centuries.
10642
Flashcards
What is the primary reason stated for the 
Maithil Brahmin migration to Bengal?
The pursuit of livelihood (Ajeevikarth).
10643
Flashcards
The genealogical records that document 
lineage blemishes or faults are 
commonly referred to as the _____.
Dushan Panji (The Black Book)
10644
Flashcards
Which major region in Bengal became a 
prominent settlement for the Maithil 
Brahmin diaspora mentioned in the text?
Malda.
10645
Flashcards
In the context of the Jalpaiguri lineage, 
who was the son of Kunjmohan?
Ravindranath.
10646
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Ravindranath 
mentioned in the Jalpaiguri records.
Ratinath and Tapan Kumar.
10647
Flashcards
What were the specific professional 
titles held by Tapan Kumar of Jalpaiguri?
Tea Estate Proprietor and Mechanical Junior 
Engineer.
10648
Flashcards
In Maithil Panji terminology, what does 
the term 'Sut' or 'Sutau' denote?
Son or sons.
10649
Flashcards
In Maithil Panji terminology, what does 
the term 'Suta' or 'Sutai' denote?
Daughter or daughters.
10650
Flashcards
According to the Naraun Sulhani 
records, who was the father of 
Munindramohan, Subodhchand, and 
Phanindramohan?
Rajender Mohan.
10651
Flashcards
Who were the three sons of Uman 
mentioned in the lineage of Bhairav?
Kamalakant, Dhirkant, and Veerkant.
10652
Flashcards
Identify the three sons of Kamalakant in 
the lineage descending from Haridev.
Ninu, Khirodhar, and Ganga Prasad.
10653
Flashcards
In the genealogy of Khirodhar, who were 
his three sons?
Chand Prasad, Krishna Prasad, and Hari Prasad.
10654
Flashcards
Who were the sons of Ganga Prasad 
mentioned in the records of Balia Ekma?
Durgay Prasad, Bhavani Prasad, and Tarani 
Prasad.
10655
Flashcards
Which maternal lineage connection does 
the term 'Dau' typically signify in Panji 
records?
The maternal grandfather's lineage.
10656
Flashcards
Who was the father of Jhumman, a 
resident of Mahalbadi?
Matar.
10657
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Damodar in the 
Sambhual lineage.
Diwakar and Madhukar.
10658
Flashcards
Who were the sons of Madhukar in the 
Sambhual lineage?
Prithvidhar and Rajdhar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
10659
Flashcards
In the lineage of Rajdhar, who were the 
three sons mentioned?
Sabhapati, Gangadhar, and Sripati.
10660
Flashcards
Who were the five sons of Sabhapati in 
the Sambhual lineage?
Vasudev, Damu, Mani, Maharath, and 
Yadunandan.
10661
Flashcards
Identify the three sons of Vasudev in the 
lineage associated with the Marad region.
Hado, Salkhan, and Goshaindash.
10662
Flashcards
Who were the two sons of Salkhan in the 
lineage of Vasudev?
Vishal and Laxminath.
10663
Flashcards
In the lineage of Goshain, who was the 
son who resided in Sakradih?
Baidyanath.
10664
Flashcards
Identify the son of Baidyanath in the 
Sambhual lineage.
Harinandan.
10665
Flashcards
Who were the six sons of Harinandan's 
son, Jeevan?
Hari, Atmaram, Mansaram, Dataram, Sahebram, 
and Lalaram.
10666
Flashcards
In the lineage of Atmaram, who were the 
two sons?
Chandidutt and Lochananand.
10667
Flashcards
Who was the son of Mansaram in the 
Sambhual lineage?
Amritnath.
10668
Flashcards
Identify the three sons of Dataram 
mentioned in the lineage of Jeevan.
Hulashdutt, Gokhulnath, and Dhirnath.
10669
Flashcards
Who were the two sons of Sahebram in 
the Sambhual lineage?
Nandkumar and Gorachand.
10670
Flashcards
In the Gorachand lineage, who were the 
two sons of the resident of Dighey?
Raju and Raghunath.
10671
Flashcards
Who was the son of Prannath in the 
Sodarpur Rohad lineage?
Nandlal.
10672
Flashcards
Identify the son of Nandlal in the 
Sodarpur Rohad lineage.
Yajneshwar.
10673
Flashcards
Who were the six sons of Yajneshwar?
Batuknath, Maheshnath, Damodar, 
Shashishekhar, Deviprasad, and Taraknath.
10674
Flashcards
In the Batuknath lineage, who were the 
three sons mentioned?
Baidyanath, Loknath, and Shaktinath.
10675
Flashcards
Identify the four sons of Damodar in the 
Sodarpur lineage.
Rajendranarayan, Vipin, Vishweshwar, and 
Ramnarayan.
10676
Flashcards
Who was the son of Taraknath in the 
Sodarpur lineage?
Gayanath.
10677
Flashcards
In the lineage of Gayanath, who were the 
two sons mentioned?
Ramaninath and Jnanendranath.
10678
Flashcards
Who was the father of Manek in the 
Pachyob Padmapur lineage?
Ramnath.
10679
Flashcards
In the Manek lineage, who were the three 
sons?
Pitho, Gangesh, and Bhavesh.
10680
Flashcards
Who were the three sons of Pitho in the 
Padmapur lineage?
Suryakar, Bhanukar, and Aditya.
10681
Flashcards
Identify the son of Bhanukar in the 
lineage of Pitho.
Ratnakar.
10682
Flashcards
Who were the three sons of Ratnakar in 
the Pachyob lineage?
Budhikar, Hore, and Dinakar.
10683
Flashcards
In the lineage of Budhikar, who was his 
son?
Shankar.
10684
Flashcards
Who were the three sons of Shankar in 
the Pachyob lineage?
Chandrakar, Vibhakar, and Veni.
10685
Flashcards
Identify the three sons of Chandrakar in 
the lineage associated with Pachyob.
Padmadhar, Pankhu, and Jivadhar.
10686
Flashcards
In the lineage of Jivadhar, who were the 
six sons mentioned?
Ram, Harinath, Srinath, Sashinath, Govindnath, 
and Padmanabh.
10687
Flashcards
Who were the two sons of Srinath in the 
Pachyob lineage?
Jagat and Bhim.
10688
Flashcards
Identify the eight sons of Jagat 
mentioned in the lineage of Srinath.
Devkrishna, Purushi, Devanand, Paramanand, 
Veni, Kanh, Vamshi, and Vadana.
10689
Flashcards
Who were the two sons of Purushi in the 
lineage of Jagat?
Vishweshwar and Janaki.
10690
Flashcards
In the Vishweshwar lineage, who were 
the two sons?
Dulli and Totanath.
10691
Flashcards
Who was the son of Dulli in the lineage 
of Purushi?
Bholanath.
10692
Flashcards
Identify the four sons of Devkrishna, 
who migrated to Malda for livelihood.
Prasadi, Chunniram, Harilal, and Kali Prasad.
10693
Flashcards
Who was the son of Chunniram in the 
lineage of Devkrishna?
Durgay Prasad.
10694
Flashcards
Identify the son of Durgay Prasad in the 
Pachyob lineage.
Dhananjay.
10695
Flashcards
In the lineage of Harilal, who were his 
two sons?
Dolgovind and Navinchand.
10696
Flashcards
Who were the three sons of Dolgovind 
in the Pachyob lineage?
Bhuvan, Darpanarayan, and Chandan.
10697
Flashcards
In the Bhuvan lineage, who were the two 
sons?
Devram and Nayan.
10698
Flashcards
Who was the son of Devram in the 
lineage of Bhuvan?
Kanram.
10699
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Kanram in the 
Pachyob lineage.
Ramsunder and Jaygopal.
10700
Flashcards
Who were the two sons of Jaygopal 
mentioned in the Pachyob lineage?
Vishnuchand and Maheshchand.
10701
Flashcards
According to entry 17 of the Dushan 
Panji, what was the status of the mother 
of Vidyapati?
Devadasi.
10702
Flashcards
According to entry 30 of the Dushan 
Panji, what caste blemish is mentioned 
in the lineage of Som and Vasudev?
Rajak (Washerman).
10703
Flashcards
In the 'Dushan Panji', entry 1 relates to 
the lineage of which village?
Sarisab.
10704
Flashcards
The village of Kankoura is associated 
with which broader lineage branch in 
Malda?
Dighve.
10705
Flashcards
Identify the specific village location in 
Malda associated with Dighve 
Sandahpur.
Ramnagar.
10706
Flashcards
Which village is listed as the settlement 
for the Naraun Shaktiraipur lineage in 
Malda?
Talaki.
10707
Flashcards
The Pachyob Padmapur lineage is 
primarily located in which sub-area of 
Malda?
Vagi Tola.
10708
Flashcards
Which village in Malda is associated 
with the lineage of Srimulah Kanti?
Raghunathpur.
10709
Flashcards
In the Dushan Panji, entry 16 refers to 
Harihar's mother as a _____.
Devadasi
10710
Flashcards
Identify the son of Baidju mentioned in 
entry 24 of the Dushan Panji.
Hariram.
10711
Flashcards
Who was the son of Hariram in the 
lineage mentioned in entry 24 of the 
Dushan Panji?
Rajballabh.
10712
Flashcards
In the Dushan Panji entry 29, what is the 
blemish associated with the lineage of 
Gopal and Madhav?
Rajaki (Washerman).
10713
Flashcards
Which location is associated with the 
'Western Country' (Pashchim Desh) 
lineage in the Dushan Panji?
Yamugam.
10714
Flashcards
In entry 27 of the Dushan Panji, which 
village is associated with the lineage of 
Jagannath?
Karmha.
10715
Flashcards
The term 'Mool' in Maithil Panji refers 
to the _____.
Original root village of a lineage
10716
Flashcards
Which lineage is linked to the village of 
Mangalwadi in the Dushan Panji?
Khandvala Guldi.
10717
Flashcards
Identify the daughter of Mahinarayan 
mentioned in the context of the Naraun 
lineage.
Subuli.
10718
Flashcards
Who was the son of Keshav mentioned 
in the lineage of Pachyob Padmapur?
Baidju.
10719
Flashcards
In the lineage of Rajballabh, who were 
his four sons?
Harijeev, Ramjeev, Lakhan, and Khadgi.
10720
Flashcards
Who were the two sons of Harijeev in 
the Pachyob lineage?
Muktaram and Khoshali.
10721
Flashcards
Identify the two sons of Muktaram in the 
lineage of Harijeev.
Natthu and Nandkumar.
10722
Flashcards
Who were the five sons of Nandkumar 
in the Pachyob lineage?
Nafarichand, Padmalochan, Vishwanath, 
Mangali, and Chanda.
10723
Flashcards
In the lineage of Nafarichand, who were 
the five sons mentioned?
Damru, Baidyanath, Baleshwar, Mahananda, and 
Deepchand.
10724
Flashcards
Who were the four sons of Padmalochan 
in the Pachyob lineage?
Sansar, Golakh, Dinbandhu, and Chandra.
10725
Flashcards
Identify the son of Vishwanath 
mentioned in the Pachyob lineage.
Doman.
10726
Flashcards
What is the primary subject of the 
document titled 'Dushana Panji' or 'The 
Black Book'?
It is a genealogical record documenting specific 
lineages and matrimonial 'defects' or exceptions 
within the Maithil Brahmin community.
10727
Flashcards
In the context of the Panji records, what 
does the term 'Matruka' denote?
It refers to the maternal lineage or the mother's 
side of the family.
10728
Flashcards
What is the meaning of the term 'Agyat' 
when it appears next to a maternal 
lineage name?
It indicates that the specific maternal lineage or 
name is unknown or unrecorded.
10729
Flashcards
The term 'Nishiddha Kula', found in 
entries like 35 and 36, translates to 
_____.
Prohibited lineage or family.
10730
Flashcards
What social phenomenon does the term 
'Antarjatiya' describe in these 
genealogical records?
Inter-caste marriage.
10731
Flashcards
Which specific village is repeatedly 
associated with the Sodarpur lineage in 
the early entries (33-35)?
Gadhapandoli.
10732
Flashcards
According to entry 33, what is the status 
of the maternal lineage for Gangadhar?
Matruka Agyat (Maternal lineage unknown).
10733
Flashcards
In entry 35, which lineage is associated 
with the status of 'Nishiddha Kula'?
Nidhi.
10734
Flashcards
Which village is linked with the 
'Budhwal' lineage in entry 36?
Darihara.
10735
Flashcards
In entry 37, the 'Sarisav' lineage is 
grouped with which other primary 
lineage?
Karmaha.
10736
Flashcards
Which specific ancestor from the 
Sodarpur lineage is mentioned in entry 
38 alongside the Mandar village?
Pashupati.
10737
Flashcards
Entry 40 lists a connection between 
'Hariam' and which village for the 
Raghupati lineage?
Darihara.
10738
Flashcards
What is the significance of entry 41 
mentioning 'Antarjatiyam' in relation to 
the lineage of Shuddi?
It records a marriage that took place outside the 
permitted caste boundaries.
10739
Flashcards
Which village is associated with the 
'Pavoli' lineage in entry 43?
Ekhara.
10740
Flashcards
In entry 45, the 'Pali' village is linked to 
which primary lineage group?
Sodarpur.
10741
Flashcards
What specific marital detail is noted for 
the 'Tanti' lineage in entry 48?
Marriage into the Tanti caste (Tanti Jati me 
Vivah).
10742
Flashcards
Entry 49 records a connection to the 
_____ community, which is traditionally 
associated with leather work.
Chammakarini.
10743
Flashcards
In entry 50, which village is associated 
with the 'Iraun' lineage?
Mandar.
10744
Flashcards
Entry 51 links the Sodarpur Pali lineage 
with the 'Khandwala' village and which 
other specific site?
Dih.
10745
Flashcards
The 'Hanidani Ruramati' lineage in entry 
52 is associated with which village 
group?
Sodarpur Mandar.
10746
Flashcards
Which female figure's daughter is 
mentioned in the inter-caste context of 
entry 54?
Rajakeshwar Suta (Daughter of Rajakeshwar).
10747
Flashcards
What specific community does the term 
'Rajak' refer to in entries 54 and 55?
The washerman community.
10748
Flashcards
In entry 56, the Sodarpur Karmaha 
lineage is noted for a marriage with a 
daughter from the _____ community.
Gopa (Yadav).
10749
Flashcards
Which village is linked to the 'Belauch' 
lineage in entry 57?
Sodarpur.
10750
Flashcards
In entry 58, the 'Hariam' lineage is 
recorded alongside which village 
associated with 'Madhav'?
Darihara.
10751
Flashcards
Which village is associated with the 
'Ghusaut' lineage in entry 59?
Sodarpur.
10752
Flashcards
Entry 60 records an inter-caste marriage 
involving a daughter of the _____ 
community from Naharua.
Chammakar (Leather worker).
10753
Flashcards
The 'Tailika Devai' mention in entry 61 
refers to a marriage into which 
community?
The Teli (oil-presser) community.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
10754
Flashcards
In entry 62, the 'Karmauli' lineage is 
associated with a marriage into the 
_____ family.
Mahapatra.
10755
Flashcards
Which community is again mentioned in 
entry 63 as being involved in an 
'Antarjatiya' union?
Chammakar (Naharua).
10756
Flashcards
Entry 64 notes a 'Rajak Parampara' 
(washerman tradition) in the context of 
the _____ lineage.
Darihara Karmaha.
10757
Flashcards
In entry 66, the wife of Shakti is noted to 
be from the _____ community.
Champo Yavani.
10758
Flashcards
Which lineage is linked to 'Jhanjharpur' 
in entry 67?
Udaypur.
10759
Flashcards
In entry 68, the 'Budhwal' lineage is 
associated with which village group?
Pavoli Satlakha.
10760
Flashcards
Entry 69 links the 'Sodarpur Karmaha' 
group with which village and the 
individual 'Gadadhar'?
Mandar.
10761
Flashcards
Which lineage is associated with the 
'Yamugam' village in entry 70?
Sodarpur Satlakha.
10762
Flashcards
The 'Valiyas Gangoli' group in entry 71 
is linked to which village?
Vithual.
10763
Flashcards
In entry 72, 'Sodarpur Budhwal' is 
grouped with which specific location?
Jagath.
10764
Flashcards
Which individual is associated with the 
'Sodarpur Baheradhi' lineage in entry 73?
Shrikar.
10765
Flashcards
What specific caste is mentioned in the 
'Darbhanga Shakha' (Darbhanga branch) 
in entry 74?
Yadav (Jaisingh).
10766
Flashcards
Entry 75 mentions 'Yadav Rahmu' in the 
context of which lineage?
Khandwala.
10767
Flashcards
Which village is linked to the 'Pandua 
Hariam' lineage in entry 76?
Mandar.
10768
Flashcards
In entry 77, the 'Sodarpur Karmaha' 
lineage is associated with which 
'Ashram'?
Simhashram.
10769
Flashcards
Entry 78 records a marriage involving 
the 'Naharua Chammakarini' and which 
individual?
Giru.
10770
Flashcards
The term 'Param Brahman' in entry 79 is 
applied to which status or title?
Mahapatra.
10771
Flashcards
In entry 80, the 'Sodarpur Baheradhi' 
lineage is linked to which village group?
Sodarpur Chakliya.
10772
Flashcards
The individuals 'Harhu' and 'Raghu' in 
entry 33 belong to which lineage?
Sodarpur Darihara.
10773
Flashcards
In entry 34, 'Lakhan' and 'Janardan' are 
descendants of which lineage?
Sodarpur Mandar.
10774
Flashcards
Which lineage is linked with 'Sakraudhi' 
in entry 35?
Sodarpur Satlakha.
10775
Flashcards
The name 'Aravind' in entries 35-37 is 
frequently associated with the label 
_____.
Nishiddha Kula (Prohibited lineage).
10776
Flashcards
Entry 37 lists 'Haridatt' and 'Vagishwar' 
under which primary lineage?
Sarisav Karmaha.
10777
Flashcards
In entry 39, 'Basau' and 'Mahai' are 
associated with which village?
Sodarpur Pali.
10778
Flashcards
Which lineage is linked to 'Udaypur' in 
entry 41?
Hariam.
10779
Flashcards
Entry 42 mentions 'Gadadhar' and 
'Ramnath' in relation to which lineage?
Sodarpur Karmaha.
10780
Flashcards
Which village is associated with the 
'Gangoli' lineage in entry 45?
Navaranga.
10781
Flashcards
Entry 46 links 'Shankar' and 'Veni' to the 
'Sodarpur Hariam' group and which 
village?
Mandar.
10782
Flashcards
In entry 47, 'Matkar' and 'Bhmaru' are 
associated with which lineage?
Sodarpur Valias.
10783
Flashcards
The 'Tanti' lineage in entry 48 is also 
linked to which village?
Sodarpur.
10784
Flashcards
Which lineage is associated with 'Dadi' 
and 'Tattvachintamani' in entry 49?
Charmakarini.
10785
Flashcards
In entry 52, 'Dalu' and 'Gadu' are 
individuals belonging to which group?
Hanidani Ruramati.
10786
Flashcards
Which village is linked to 'Iraun Mandar' 
in entry 53?
Jamuni.
10787
Flashcards
Entry 54 records 'Sadhukar' and 'Shrikar' 
under the _____ lineage.
Khaual Vaheradhi.
10788
Flashcards
In entry 55, the 'Khaual Vahoradhi' 
lineage is linked to a marriage with a 
daughter from the _____ caste.
Rajak (Washerman).
10789
Flashcards
Which lineage is associated with 
'Vamdev' and 'Raghunath' in entry 56?
Sodarpur Karmaha.
10790
Flashcards
In entry 57, 'Guni' and 'Shankar' are 
linked to which village?
Sodarpur Belauch.
10791
Flashcards
Entry 59 mentions 'Ram' and 
'Krishnadev' in the context of which 
group?
Sodarpur Ghusaut.
10792
Flashcards
Which individual is linked to the 
'Charmakar Naharua' community in 
entry 60?
Nagar.
10793
Flashcards
In entry 61, 'Mahipati' and 'Kanh' are 
associated with the _____ village group.
Khandwala Thataunt.
10794
Flashcards
Entry 63 links 'Shiru' and 'Matu' with the 
'Budhwal' lineage and which other 
location?
Sakraudhi Pali.
10795
Flashcards
Which lineage is linked to 'Someshwar' 
and 'Sureshwar' in entry 65?
Rajak Parampara (washerman tradition).
10796
Flashcards
In entry 66, 'Krishnapathi' and 
'Suryapathi' are associated with the 
_____ lineage.
Mandar Pachahi.
10797
Flashcards
Entry 67 links 'Bhave' and 'Raghupati' to 
the 'Rajak' tradition and which village?
Sodarpur Hariam.
10798
Flashcards
Which individuals are linked to the 
'Sodarpur Karmaha' group in entry 69?
Gadadhar and Visho.
10799
Flashcards
In entry 70, 'Harikesh' and 'Kashi' are 
associated with the _____ lineage.
Sodarpur Satlakha.
10800
Flashcards
Entry 74 mentions 'Shivapathi' and 
'Yagyapathi' in the context of the _____ 
community.
Yadav.
10801
Flashcards
Which individual is named as a 
'MahapÄ tra' (Param Brahmin) in entry 
79?
Hore.
10802
Flashcards
Which village is linked to the 'Khaual' 
lineage in entry 79?
Darihara.
10803
Flashcards
In entry 75, the wife of Hrishikesh is 
noted as being from the _____ caste.
Yadav.
10804
Flashcards
Which individual is linked to 
'Gadhapandoli' in entry 36?
Ratir Siddhu.
10805
Flashcards
What is the status of the 'maternal 
unknown' record for JÅ«á¸ 
ÄuÃ± in 
entry 33?
Matruka Agyat.
10806
Flashcards
The 'Darihara' village is linked to the 
'Ghusaut' lineage in which numbered 
entry?
33
10807
Flashcards
What is the English translation of the 
title 'Dushana Panji' used in the source 
material?
The Black Book
10808
Flashcards
Who is the author of the provided 
excerpts from 'Dushana Panji'?
Gajendra Thakur
10809
Flashcards
In the context of Maithil Brahmin 
genealogy, what does the term 'Dushana' 
signify?
A blemish or social/ritual fault within a lineage, 
typically resulting from an irregular marriage.
10810
Flashcards
According to the 'Vyakhya' 
(Explanation) section, how many 
generations does it typically take for a 
'Dushana' lineage to become 'Shuddha' 
(pure)?
Six generations (attaining purity in the sixth 
generation).
10811
Flashcards
Which historical figure's lineage is used 
as an example of reaching 'Shuddha' 
status in the sixth generation?
Devdhar
10812
Flashcards
What specific social fault is associated 
with Bhatt Bhushan of the Mandar 
Marar lineage in entry 83?
Marriage with a 'Guru-patni' (a teacher's wife).
10813
Flashcards
The entry for Afel (Entry 87) records a 
'Dushana' involving marriage with a 
woman from which background?
A 'Yavani' (foreigner or Muslim woman), 
specifically named Daulat Jahan.
10814
Flashcards
What is the name of the 'Yavani' woman 
mentioned in the blemish involving the 
Sodarpur Khaual lineage?
Daulat Jahan
10815
Flashcards
In entry 94, what type of woman is 
'Vartini', leading to a lineage blemish?
A widow ('Vartini Vidhwa').
10816
Flashcards
According to entry 96, what was the 
status of the woman 'Dheevar Dasi' who 
married into the Kujouli Mandar 
lineage?
A slave or servant from the fisherman caste.
10817
Flashcards
What does the term 'Path-patita kanya' 
refer to in the record of blemishes?
A girl who has fallen from her social or moral 
path/caste status.
10818
Flashcards
Which philosopher, known for writing 
the 'Tattva-Chintamani', is mentioned in 
a 'Dushana' involving a 'Chamarkarini' 
(leatherworker woman)?
Gangesha (Gangesh Upadhyaya).
10819
Flashcards
According to the text, how many years 
after his father's death was Gangesha 
allegedly born?
Five years.
10820
Flashcards
What is a 'Mool' in the context of these 
genealogical records?
An ancestral village or the root lineage identity 
of a Maithil Brahmin.
10821
Flashcards
List three examples of Maithil Brahmin 
'Mools' mentioned in the text.
Sodarpur, Mandar, and Karmaha.
10822
Flashcards
The 'Dushana' involving 'Gop-vivah' 
(Entry 97) refers to marriage with a 
woman from which caste?
The Gop (cowherd) caste.
10823
Flashcards
What is the 'Dushana' associated with 
the 'Koch Rajvanshi' kings of Assam in 
these records?
Lineage blemishes resulting from marriages or 
associations with the Koch royal family.
10824
Flashcards
In entry 85, a blemish is recorded for a 
marriage with a girl described as _____.
Path-patita kanya (a fallen girl).
10825
Flashcards
The term 'Agyat' often appears in the 
mother's column in the Panji; what does 
this indicate?
The identity or lineage of the mother is unknown 
or not recorded.
10826
Flashcards
Which lineage is associated with the 
'Khandavala' Mool in entry 82?
Khandavala Karmaha.
10827
Flashcards
Entry 90 mentions people arriving from 
which direction as a source of lineage 
concern?
The West ('Paschim desh-sa agat').
10828
Flashcards
What marker or sign ('Chinha') is used in 
the Panji to denote a 'Param Brahmin' 
(Maha-pataka) daughter's offspring?
A specific symbol or 'Chinha' is placed in the 
records to identify them as descendants of a 
'great sinner'.
10829
Flashcards
Who is the son of Gangu in the 
Baheradhi lineage, mentioned in the 
explanation section?
Do.
10830
Flashcards
In the Sodarpur lineage, Parashurama is 
the son of which individual?
Govinda.
10831
Flashcards
The blemish involving 'Rajak Kanya' 
(Entry 26) refers to a woman from 
which profession/caste?
Washerman (Rajak).
10832
Flashcards
The 'Dushana' involving 'Chamarkarini' 
(Leatherworker) is associated with 
which name in the explanation?
Ananda.
10833
Flashcards
Which text is Gangesha credited with 
writing in the genealogical 'Vyakhya'?
Tattva-Chintamani.
10834
Flashcards
According to the list, what is the Mool 
of the person named Jasu in entry 86?
Sodarpur Khoual.
10835
Flashcards
Which entry number corresponds to the 
'Kerwal Shakha' (Kerwal branch)?
Entry 92.
10836
Flashcards
In the records, what does 'Mataui' likely 
refer to in the context of village lists?
A specific maternal village or sub-location 
associated with the lineage.
10837
Flashcards
The blemish involving the 'Mandar' 
Mool and 'Sawai's son Surasar' is 
categorized under which type of 
irregular marriage?
It is listed as a specific 'Chinha' (mark) of 
blemish in the explanation notes.
10838
Flashcards
Name the individual from the 'Kujouli' 
Mool who married a 'Dheevar Dasi'.
Mandal.
10839
Flashcards
In entry 95, the lineage 'Hariam' is 
associated with which primary Mool?
Sodarpur.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
10840
Flashcards
Which lineage is linked to the location 
'Sarisav' in entry 90?
Karmaha.
10841
Flashcards
The name 'Daulat Jahan' represents a 
blemish of what religious/ethnic nature 
in this Brahmin record?
Yavani (Muslim).
10842
Flashcards
What is the father's name of the person 
'Gangadhar' mentioned in the 
explanation of the first example?
Camru (of the Valiyas-mool).
10843
Flashcards
To which Mool does the person 'Goge' 
belong in the purification example?
Sakrarhi.
10844
Flashcards
The purification of the lineage was 
complete when Devdhar's mother was 
from which Mool?
Irawal (or Karwal).
10845
Flashcards
In entry 88, which Mool is mentioned 
alongside 'Mahishi' and 'Budhwal'?
Sodarpur.
10846
Flashcards
The term 'Maha-pataka' used in the notes 
refers to what?
A 'great sin' or a person who has committed a 
severe social/religious transgression.
10847
Flashcards
Which 'Mool' is associated with entry 
216/I (Entry 86)?
Sodarpur Khoual.
10848
Flashcards
What is the significance of the village 
'Darihara' in these records?
It is one of the frequently cited 'Mools' or 
villages where various lineages are based.
10849
Flashcards
The blemish associated with 'Asamaka 
Koch Rajvanshi' is linked to a daughter 
named _____.
Sohago.
10850
Flashcards
In the explanation, the marriage of 
'Jivadhar's son Gahai' is linked to which 
Mool?
Tankbal.
10851
Flashcards
Which individual from the 'Karmaha' 
Mool married 'Harilata'?
Raghunath's son Harinath.
10852
Flashcards
The 'Vartini Vidhwa' (remarried widow) 
blemish in entry 94 is specifically linked 
to which Mool?
Pali.
10853
Flashcards
Who is the son of 'Sudarshan' mentioned 
in the Sakrarhi lineage context?
Madhav.
10854
Flashcards
The blemish involving a 'Rajak' 
(washerman) girl 'Runamathi' is 
associated with which person's 
descendants?
Hadir-i (or similar phonetic name in the text).
10855
Flashcards
In entry 89, which Mool is listed first in 
the sequence of 'Iroun', 'Pali', and 
'Darihara'?
Iroun (or Irauri).
10856
Flashcards
The name 'Sodarpur Belaunch' appears 
in which entry number?
Entry 85 (206/II).
10857
Flashcards
Which Mool is associated with 
'Panchobh' and 'Pavauli' in entry 88?
Mahishi.
10858
Flashcards
According to the text, the child of a 
'Chamarkar' (leatherworker)[ 
'Gangesha's father'], Gangesha was born 
how many years after the Brahmin 
father's death/ absence?
Five years.
10859
Flashcards
Which specific branch of the 'Maithil' 
Brahmins is the primary subject of the 
'Dushana Panji'?
Those recorded in the Panji Prabandha 
(specifically the lineage records of the Mithila 
region).
10860
Flashcards
What is the status of the 'Shuddha' child 
in the final stage of the genealogical 
example?
Fully purified ('Purnatah Shuddha').
10861
Flashcards
Entry 91 lists 'Brahmapura' and 'Purahi' 
alongside which primary Mool?
Sodarpur Pali.
10862
Flashcards
The blemish 'Yadava Rahmuka Putre' 
involves a marriage with a woman from 
which community?
The Yadava community (cowherds/Gop).
10863
Flashcards
In entry 81, 'Budhwal Mandar' is 
associated with which specific location?
Kujouli.
10864
Flashcards
Who is the 'Gajendra Thakur' mentioned 
in the document footer?
The author or editor of this compilation of the 
'Dushana Panji'.
10865
Flashcards
In entry 93, the 'Sodarpur Darihara' 
lineage is noted for a marriage with a 
girl from which background?
A 'Path-patita kanya' (fallen girl) from the 
Gangoli lineage.
10866
Flashcards
What is the primary purpose of the 
'Dushana Panji' records?
To track and preserve the history of 'faults' or 
'blemishes' in lineages to prevent ritually 
'impure' marriages.
10867
Flashcards
The term 'Mataui' (à¤®à¤¤à¤¾à¤‰à¤¿) 
used in entries like 81 and 84 likely 
refers to _____.
Maternal ancestry or the maternal village 
connection.
10868
Flashcards
Which 'Mool' is associated with the 
person 'Ratipati's son Balbhar'?
Sodarpur.
10869
Flashcards
The entry 227 (Entry 88) mentions 
'Mahishi' as the primary location; which 
region is this historically part of?
Mithila.
10870
Flashcards
What 'Chinha' is specifically linked to 
the 'Asamaka Koch Rajvanshi' in the 
explanation?
The blemish mark associated with the daughter 
named Sohago.
10871
Flashcards
In entry 94, the 'Vartini Vidhwa' is 
described as the child of whom?
Ganapati Chand.
10872

Gangu. Great-grandson: Goge; Daughter married Kanhu. Great-great-
grandson: Rud; Daughter married Helu. Great-great-great-grandson: 
Chand; Daughter married Horai. Great-great-great-great-grandson: 
Ram; Daughter married Chand. Sixth generation son: Devadhar.

Matrik (Maternal side)

Suta
Daughter; indicates the strategic transfer of 
social capital between lineages.
11247
05_data 1-
2_Sheet2
Patni
Wife; denotes the maternal lineage entering a 
specific branch.
11248
05_data 1-
2_Sheet2
Siddhanta
The formal genealogical approval for a marriage 
alliance.
11249
06_data 1-
2_Sheet1
suto (Suto)
Masculine
Indicates "Son of." It identifies a male descendant and links him 
directly to the father named immediately before him.
11250
06_data 1-
2_Sheet1
sutā (Suta)
Feminine
Indicates "Daughter of." This marker is essential for tracking the 
movement of lineages into new families via marriage.
11251
06_data 1-
2_Sheet1
pui/puiī (Puri/Puri)
Masc. / Fem.
Historical shorthand for Putra (Son) or Putri (Daughter). Crucially, 
this often signifies a child’s link to a specific mother’s name (e.g., 
Maghavi Puri ), providing a "horizontal" matrilineal link within a 
patrilineal record.
11252
06_data 1-
2_Sheet2
prao (Pr.)
Prapautra
Literally "Great-grandson." Used as shorthand to skip intermediate 
generations or to denote a specific alias/branch (e.g., Pr. Vikalasyaiva 
).
11253
06_data 1-
2_Sheet2
uo ṭīo / uo ṭīo (U. Ti.)
Up-Tipa
"Sub-note." Indicates a branch or supplementary commentary that has 
been added to the main village record.
11254
06_data 1-
2_Sheet2
mīmāṃsaka (Mimamsaka)
Mimamsa Scholar
Denotes a specialist in Hermeneutics and Philosophy. Titles like 
Mimamsaka Hari signal the high intellectual status of that lineage.
11255
06_data 1-
2_Sheet2
vaiyākaraṇa (Vaiyakarana)
Grammarian
Indicates an expert in linguistics and Sanskrit grammar, a hallmark of 
Mithila’s scholarly elite.
11256
06_data 1-
2_Sheet2
mao mao (Ma. Ma.)
Mahamahopadhyaya
"Greatest of Great Teachers." The highest scholarly title, identifying 
the most prestigious figures in the family line.
11257
