08_data 1-
2_Sheet1
Suta
suta
Son
The most common marker for a male child, usually appearing 
immediately after the father's name.
11293
08_data 1-
2_Sheet1
Sutā
sutā
Daughter / Sons (Plural)
Note the Ambiguity: While literally "daughter," in Panji shorthand, it 
is frequently used as a plural marker (Sutāh) to introduce a list of 
multiple sons.
11294
08_data 1-
2_Sheet1
Sutau
sutau
Two Sons
The dual "au" suffix is a precise Sanskrit grammatical tool used here 
to prevent future assumption of missing brothers; it explicitly 
confirms exactly two sons existed.
11295
08_data 1-
2_Sheet1
Putra
putra
Son
Often used interchangeably with Suta , though sometimes used to 
highlight a specific branch or the beginning of a maternal subsection 
(e.g., Hemavati Putra ).

Era of King Harasimhadeva (c. 1326 CE) Establishment of the formalized Panji-Prabandh . Gunakar Jha (Brahmins); Shankar Dutt (Kayasthas); Vijay Dutt 
(Kshatriyas).
Transitioned oral lineage tracking into a mandatory, evidence-based 
state audit.

32_data_Sheet
1
Era of King Madhava Singh (c. 1760 CE) Order for "Shakha Panji" (Branch Records).
Formalization of Shrotriya, Jog, and Panjibaddha tiers.
Introduced granular hierarchy, allowing for specialized tracking of 
elite and evolving lineages.

33_data_Sheet
1
Mool Panji
The foundational, original records of the 
primary families.
Acts as the "root" source that anchors every subsequent family 
branch.
11734
33_data_Sheet
1
Shakha Panji
Branch records ordered by King Madhav Singh 
(c. 1760 AD) for specific social categories.
Allows the system to evolve and manage sub-communities (Sotriya, 
Jog, etc.) as the population grew.
11735
33_data_Sheet
1

Tracks the 20 ancient clans and their original 
ancestral sages (Rishis).

1

ancestors originated.

33_data_Sheet
1

The "Black Book" documenting lineage erosion 
or social shifts.
An unflinching record of transparency that protects future 
generations from unknown kinship defects.

33_data_Sheet
1

1
Valiyasa
Son named Gangadhar born to Chamaru and Gunawati Himsha. 
Gangadhar's daughter married Nathu (Sarisav Mool), son Visho. 
Visho's daughter married Gangu (Sakaradhi Mool), son Goge. 
Goge's daughter married Kanhu (Panichobh Mool), son Rud. Rud's 
daughter married Helu (Darihara Mool), son Chand. Chand's 
daughter married Horai (Pali Mool), son Ram. Ram's daughter 
married Chand (Narwal Mool), son Devadhar.
Gunawati Himsha belonged to a different caste (Aan Jatik); the 
lineage became completely pure (Purnatah Shuddha) only in the sixth 
generation with Devadhar.
1
11763
37_data_Sheet
1
2
Budhwal
Descendants of Madhav. Madhav's son Bhim had a daughter whose 
marriage details are recorded.
Marriage of Madhav's granddaughter (Bhim's daughter) is noted as a 
lineage defect affecting many households.
1

married Gangu (Sakaradhi Mool), son Goge. Goge's 
daughter married Kanhu (Panichobh Mool), son Rud. 
Rud's daughter married Helu (Darihara Mool), son 
Chand. Chand's daughter married Horai (Pali Mool), 
son Ram. Ram's daughter married Chand (Narwal 
Mool), son Devadhar.
Gunawati Himsha belonged to a different caste (Aan 
Jatik); the lineage became completely pure (Purnatah 
Shuddha) only in the sixth generation with Devadhar.

records.label: पञ्जी PDF 14 — खण्ड 1 (block 350–690, IAST)
records.text:
REFERENCE — Glossary-Terms
Term
Definition
Beeji Purush
The founding ancestor of a specific lineage or Mulu.
Chhandog
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Kauthuma branch of 
the Samaveda.
Dushun Panji
A register of "faults" or deviations within a lineage, 
such as prohibited marriages.
Gotra
A clan or lineage descending from one of the primary 
ancient sages (e.g., Shandilya, Vatsa).
Jaibar
A category of Maithil Brahmins who did not initially 
maintain formalized Panji records or whose records 
were less prestigious.
Jati Shuddhi
The concept of maintaining the purity of the caste or 
lineage.
Mulu
The "root" or original lineage of a family.
Mulu Gram
The original ancestral village associated with a specific 
Mulu.
Panji
The compiled genealogical records or books of the 
Maithil community.
Panjibadh
A category of individuals whose names and lineages 
are officially recorded in the Panji books.
Panjikar
A professional genealogist authorized to maintain 
records and verify marriage eligibility.
Pravara
Illustrious ancestors within a Gotra, typically sages 
who composed Vedic hymns.
Sapinda
Relatives connected by the same "pinda" 
(body/lineage); marriage within specific degrees of 
Sapinda relationship is prohibited.
Shaktri
The formal investigation of ancestry (6 or 7 
generations) conducted before issuing a Siddhanta.
Shrotriya
The highest social tier of Maithil Brahmins, noted for 
their strict adherence to rituals and lineage purity.
Siddhanta
A formal clearance certificate issued by a Panjikar, 
permitting a marriage to proceed.
Tirhuta
The traditional script of the Maithili language, used for 
all historical Panji manuscripts.
Uderh
The comprehensive genealogical profile of an 
individual used to check marriage compatibility.
Vajasaneyi
Maithil Brahmins who follow the Madhyandina branch 
of the Yajurveda.
Yogya
A tier of Maithil Brahmins ranking below Shrotriya but 
above Panjibadh.
